name: Grover Washington Jr.
abstract: Grover Washington Jr. (December 12, 1943 – December 17, 1999) was an American jazz-funk and soul-jazz saxophonist and Grammy Award winner.

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Saxophone Summit
A Tribute to Greatness: The Sullivan Years - A Gala Celebration Honoring the Reverend Leon Sullivan

works.t: I’m Beginning to See the Light
I Got It Bad (and That Ain't Good)
Black, Brown and Beige: Come Sunday

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Blues in Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: Grover became a dear friend. Just great guys you know. Look, we had so much in common, our love for the saxophone and music and jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: I had a chance to work with Kathleen on her latest... in which she did a lot of very different... I played on it, Cyrus Chestnut played piano, James Carter plays saxophone and Grover Washington
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I got the chance to do things with Straight Life and First Light with the stable of musicians that Creed had there it was: Freddie Hubbard, Grover Washington, Jr., George Benson…
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: There was also Sonny Rollins, and there was also – so, they didn’t have the library. So they said, you can bring your own library... when you get people to listen to Grover Washington, for them to know about Ben Webster...
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: You grew up in a time where you could have, in your early years as a saxophonist, you could have been listening to Grover Washington and that kind of saxophone, but that didn’t get you, huh? No it didn’t.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: I bought everything that had a saxophone on it. And I didn’t know, you know I had everything from of course Charlie Parker, Phil Woods, Ornette Coleman, then Grover Washington Jr., Coltrane
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023
